name: Intrinsic Value of a Call Option
short_description: Calculates the intrinsic value of a call option by comparing the underlying asset's spot price (S) with the strike price (K).

inputs.name: S
inputs.description: Spot price of the underlying asset

inputs.name: K
inputs.description: Strike price of the call option

name: Intrinsic Value of a Put Option
short_description: Calculates the intrinsic value of a put option by comparing the strike price (K) and the spot price (S), taking the maximum of (K-S) and zero.

inputs.name: S
inputs.description: Spot price of the underlying asset

inputs.name: K
inputs.description: Strike price of the put option

name: 1-Period Binomial Call Price
short_description: Calculates the price of a call option using a 1-period binomial model, involving payoffs in up/down states, risk-neutral probability, and present value of the expected payoff.

inputs.name: r
inputs.description: Risk-free interest rate as a decimal

name: ROI on a Long Call Position
short_description: Calculates the Return on Investment (ROI) for a long call option position, considering the strike price, premium, and underlying asset price at expiration.

inputs.name: K
inputs.description: Strike price of the call option

inputs.name: premium
inputs.description: Upfront cost (premium) paid for the call option

inputs.name: S_T
inputs.description: Price of the underlying asset at expiration

name: Black-Scholes Call Option Price
short_description: Calculates the theoretical price of a European call option using the Black-Scholes model, considering the underlying asset's current price, strike price, risk-free interest rate, volatility, and time to expiration.

inputs.name: r
inputs.description: Risk-free interest rate (annualized, as a decimal)

inputs.name: sigma
inputs.description: Annualized volatility of the underlying asset (as a decimal)
